mission.title: minio: remove a bucket from gui?
mission.description: remove a bucket from gui? . This issue affects 15+ users.

steps.title: Identify minio troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your minio deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n minio-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio -n minio-operator --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review minio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant minio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n minio-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
kubectl get configmap -n minio-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=minio
```
In the minio webapp, why there is no way to delete a bucket from the graphical interface?

steps.title: Apply the fix for remove a bucket from gui?
steps.description:
This PR (two commits, one functionality + one UI from  ) adds the ability to delete a bucket from the web UI. The bucket must be empty for the `DeleteBucket` RPC call to succeed. There is no confirmation on the UI because deleting an empty bucket is reversible (just create a new one).

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm remove a bucket from gui?  is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio -n minio-operator --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n minio-operator --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: This PR (two commits, one functionality + one UI from  ) adds the ability to delete a bucket from the web UI. The bucket must be empty for the `DeleteBucket` RPC call to succeed. There is no confirmation on the UI because deleting an empty bucket is reversible (just create a new one).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with minio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
